target: CAT-005 Recommended Proposal Package — V51.1 Refraction Cascade
topic: Proposal-package review: new Section 1.7, cascade_dependent tag, twelve-WIN verdict flip table
reviewer_note: This is a proposal-package review (per curmudgeon.md Step 0b.4). No prose exists yet; the analyst has deliberately deferred Section 1.7 writing until curmudgeon signs off on structural placement, tag naming, and the verdict-flip table. My job is to gate prose writing, not to critique prose.
accept_proceed_with: Structural placement in Part 1 as Section 1.7 (Option a) — correct home for a geometric-invariant critique. Adjacent to Section 1.5 (aetheric refraction) and 1.6 (dielectric foundation) is the right neighborhood.

Writing the cascade argument to be STRUCTURAL rather than version-specific (analyst's own response to Defense 3). This is load-bearing and should be stated explicitly in the first paragraph of Section 1.7: the cascade is a geometric invariant of any flat-disc model where the sun must sit below a firmament ceiling AND produce observed altitudes; V51.1 is just one concrete instantiation.
Retention policy for flipped WINs (keep existing data rebuttals as secondary, add cascade as primary). No data is deleted. Good.

Companion numerics file (EXP-050-numerics.json) as a HARD PRE-REQUISITE for any wins.json patches. Must reproduce 13-27°, 1,975-3,618 km, 400-675x n(r) ratio, 83% angular modulation, and 3-8x tidal modulation from first principles with source file line references. I will NOT sign off on prose writing until this file lands and I can verify the arithmetic line by line.

Kernel-of-truth framing — the analyst correctly identifies that the V51.1 split is a serious engineering response, not a lazy fudge. Section 1.7 must preserve this tone or we violate the review's Principle 6 (charitable engagement).

holes_found.description: The verdict-flip table proposes flips for WIN-010 and WIN-017 based on incorrect characterization of those WINs' actual topics. This is the single most serious problem in the proposal and would lead directly to bad patches if acted on.

details.analyst_characterization: solar analemma or daylight-hours prediction (needs verification)
details.actual_topic: BOU 2017 eclipse -10.9 nT magnetic perturbation, explained by Chapman (1933) ionospheric conductivity mechanism
details.problem: WIN-010 is an eclipse magnetometer measurement, NOT a sun-altitude or daylight-hours claim. The cascade (which attacks sun physical vs. apparent altitude) does not reach WIN-010's argument at all. The existing 'Std Model Explains' verdict (Chapman ionospheric mechanism) is the correct framing and the cascade is not relevant here.
details.recommendation: REJECT flip. Remove WIN-010 from the cascade_dependent roster entirely. Do not cite cascade even as supplementary.

details.actual_topic: Stellar parallax reframed as 'firmament wobble' — refuted directly by Gaia DR3 1.8B-star parallax measurements showing parallax inversely proportional to distance, which a rigid firmament wobble cannot produce
details.problem: WIN-017 has nothing to do with survey triangulation baselines. It is a stellar-parallax WIN whose 'Refuted by Data' verdict via Gaia is already one of the strongest standalone rebuttals in the review. Flipping it to 'Self-Contradicted' via 'universal triangulation displacement' is both factually wrong about the WIN topic and strictly weaker than the Gaia argument it would replace.
details.recommendation: REJECT flip. WIN-017 stays 'Refuted by Data' — Gaia. No cascade reference. If there is a desire to cite cascade as supplementary, it would only be via Point 4 'universal displacement' targeting the same n(r) that bends sunlight by 13-27° would bend starlight equally — but that is already implicitly carried by Gaia refuting microarcsecond angular displacements.

holes_found.root_cause: The proposal package explicitly states in Section 6 (unknowns and risks) that moderate-confidence flips for WIN-010, 017, 018, 019, 026 were made WITHOUT re-reading each WIN's current detail_evidence. The analyst should not have included flip recommendations for WINs whose topics were unverified. The mitigation plan ('pre-commit audit step') is not enough — the audit should have happened before the proposal was routed to curmudgeon, because it led curmudgeon to spend review budget on flips that were never viable.
holes_found.recommendation: Analyst must re-read the current detail_evidence for EVERY proposed flip WIN (not just the five flagged ones) and confirm the cascade actually reaches that WIN's specific argument before the table is re-routed to curmudgeon. Confirmed-wrong flips (010, 017) are dropped from the table, not downgraded.

holes_found.description: WIN-044 is rated 'strong' confidence but the rationale does not survive scrutiny.

details.actual_topic_of_win_044: Firmament Scaling Function (FSF) from V12 geometry — internal-model derivation of 9 station-specific eclipse-magnetic predictions (E001-E009) that the dome will test against INTERMAGNET data at the August 2026 eclipse. Current verdict 'Misleading' because it is an internal derivation claimed as a confirmed WIN before the observational test exists.
details.problem: WIN-044 is about eclipse-magnetic amplitude predictions and internal self-consistency, not about sun altitude refraction. The cascade has no direct connection to the FSF. The existing 'Misleading' verdict (internal derivation claimed as confirmed pre-test) is the correct framing and is independent of the cascade.
details.recommendation: DOWNGRADE to 'stay put' (keep Misleading, no cascade reference). The 'strong' confidence rating here is unjustified.

holes_found.description: WIN-018 (analemma day length 6.9 min) and WIN-019 (analemma loop ratio 2.66) flips to Self-Contradicted are plausible in principle but the cascade linkage is indirect.

details.plausible_link: Analemma geometry does depend on sun altitude through the year. The cascade's 83% seasonal modulation of physical sun altitude would indeed make the analemma geometry incoherent — but only if the dome's analemma predictions are derived from physical altitude rather than adopted from globe astronomy.
details.concern: The dome model has a history of silently adopting globe formulas (see our Part 2b code analysis, and the curmudgeon Cycle 1 finding that WIN-018/019 are derived from globe axial tilt of 23.45°). If the dome's analemma values are post-hoc globe adoptions, the cascade does not actually reach them — the values are not derived from the dome's own sun altitude at all. The existing 'Misleading' verdict (derived from globe, not dome) is therefore STRONGER than a Self-Contradicted flip via the cascade.
details.recommendation: Analyst must verify whether WIN-018/019 detail_evidence shows dome-derived vs. globe-adopted analemma values. If globe-adopted (which is my prior), keep Misleading and do not cite cascade. If dome-derived, the flip is reasonable. Default to stay-put unless verification is done.

holes_found.description: WIN-026 (crepuscular ray divergence) is listed as moderate confidence with cascade Point 4 or Point 6 — but neither is a tight match.

details.actual_topic: Crepuscular/anticrepuscular rays converge near the antisolar point, which is impossible under a local sun but trivially explained by a sun at 1 AU producing parallel rays. Current verdict 'Refuted by Data'.
details.cascade_linkage: Point 4 (universal displacement) does not rescue the cascade here — refraction cannot turn diverging rays from a local source into converging parallel rays to the antisolar point, regardless of angle. Point 6 (eclipse geometry) is unrelated.
details.recommendation: DOWNGRADE to stay-put. Keep 'Refuted by Data' (converging rays ⇒ distant sun). No cascade reference.

holes_found.description: The proposed 'cascade_dependent: boolean' field conflates 'primary dependence' and 'secondary dependence' under a single true value. This is lossy and will create trouble downstream.

details.problem: Section 1.7's prose needs to say things like 'X WINs are primarily carried by the cascade, Y WINs secondarily cite it' — but a single boolean gives one count, not two. Curmudgeon Phase 2 HOLISTIC-CROSSREF and HOLISTIC-COMPLETENESS checks in future cycles would also benefit from knowing whether a cascade cross-reference is structural or supplementary.
better_shapes: Enum: cascade_role: 'primary' | 'secondary' | null (3 values). Cleanest for counts.
Two booleans: cascade_primary, cascade_secondary. More verbose but easy for existing schema patterns.
Object: cascade: { role: 'primary'|'secondary'|null, section_ref: '1.7' }. Most extensible, most schema change.
details.recommendation: Go with the enum (cascade_role). It matches how the prose will actually use the data and only costs one additional placeholder (CA_CASCADE_PRIMARY and CA_CASCADE_SECONDARY vs. single CA_CASCADE_DEPENDENT).

holes_found.description: The cascade's core numbers (13-27° refraction angle, 400-675x n(r) ratio, 83% angular modulation, 3-8x tidal modulation) are stated as 'approximate ranges' and deferred to a follow-up EXP-050-numerics.json file that does not yet exist. Until those numbers are independently reproduced from raw-text/ files, the cascade is only as strong as the analyst's initial CAT-005 draft arithmetic.

specific_values_needing_verification: Physical sun altitude range 1,975-3,618 km — what source file gives these endpoints? Is the 1,975 km winter-solstice value a dome-stated number or an analyst computation from a stated formula?

13-27° refraction angle — derived from what observer latitude assumption? What is the geometric formula used? Does it account for the dome's curved-ray refraction model or does it assume straight rays?

400-675x n(r) ratio — what n(r) value for starlight at the same radius is the denominator? The analyst says 0.04° — but at what altitude and wavelength? The dome's n(r) formula is n(r) = 1 + 0.20 × (8537/H(r) − 1); plugging in concrete numbers must land on the starlight value cited.
83% angular modulation — 83% of what reference value? Peak-to-trough of angular diameter ratio? Winter diameter divided by summer diameter?
3-8x tidal modulation — 3x of what, over what period? Based on r^-3 scaling of tidal force and the physical altitude range, or based on some other formula?
details.recommendation: EXP-050-numerics.json must exist and be curmudgeon-validated BEFORE any wins.json patches land. It is acceptable to write Section 1.7 prose in parallel with the numerics file (so both can be reviewed together), but the flip table must not be applied until the numerics hold up. I will block sign-off on the final patches if numerics are not reproducible.

holes_found.description: Defense 3 (moving-target critique) has more bite than the analyst's response admits. The proposal's response ('write the section structurally') is correct in principle but the proposed outline does not actually do this.

details.problem: Section 3 of the proposal ('section_outline') leads with 'The Fix: physical vs. apparent sun altitude' as Point 1 — this is V51.1-specific framing. A reader hitting Point 1 sees a version-specific patch critique, not a structural invariant. If V52 rewrites the patch, Point 1 dates immediately and a hostile reader can wave the whole section away as 'outdated critique of a superseded version'.
details.recommendation: Restructure the outline so Point 1 is 'The Geometric Invariant: Why Any Flat-Disc Model With the Dome's Constraints Must Either Break the Firmament Ceiling or Require Orders-of-Magnitude Refraction.' State the invariant first, show V51.1 as the current instance, then walk through the four-way contradiction. This survives V52, V53, any future version that keeps the disc_radius and H(r) formulas fixed. The current outline order inverts this — specific first, structural last (Point 7 'self-defeating fix' is a summary, not a framing).
details.severity_rationale: Elevated to major because the proposal's own 'the_other_side' section identifies Defense 3 as the strongest dome defense, and the current outline does not preempt it despite saying it should.

holes_found.description: The proposal acknowledges overlap with Section 1.5 (aetheric refraction as unfalsifiable escape hatch) and promises 'tight editing' to avoid duplication, but does not specify the split.

details.concern: Without a clean split, both sections will say substantially the same thing and Curmudgeon HOLISTIC-REDUNDANCY will flag it in the next cycle. Worse, a reader who encounters Section 1.5 first may dismiss Section 1.7 as 'more of the same' before getting to the numerical teeth.
details.recommended_split: Section 1.5 = general case (aetheric refraction as a structural escape hatch that can be deployed to rescue any observational discrepancy, with the dome's generic n(r) formula as exhibit A). Section 1.7 = concrete numerical case (the specific 13-27° requirement for the V51.1 sun split, the n(r) inconsistency between stars and sun, the seasonal modulation contradictions). Section 1.5 frames the move as a general pattern; Section 1.7 is the case study where the move breaks down quantitatively. Cross-reference: 1.5 forward-refs to 1.7 at the end; 1.7 back-refs to 1.5 at the top.
details.recommendation: Before writing Section 1.7 prose, the analyst must produce a one-paragraph split-statement saying which arguments live in 1.5 vs. 1.7 and get curmudgeon sign-off on that before any prose is drafted.

holes_found.description: The 'option_hybrid' suggestion (Section 1.7 primary + Section 7 kill-shot card) is mentioned but not specified. If adopted, it needs a concrete word budget and content plan.
holes_found.recommendation: DO adopt the hybrid. The kill-shot card in Section 7 should be ~150-200 words, title something like 'The V51.1 Patch Cascade: One Fix, Four New Contradictions,' with a single-sentence summary of each contradiction and a prominent link to Section 1.7 for the full argument. This gives visual prominence without duplicating the detailed numerics. Analyst must include this card content plan in the next proposal revision before writing prose.

holes_found.description: WIN-016 'Refuted by Data + Self-Contradicted (dual)' proposal — the current review verdict taxonomy does NOT support dual labels. wins.json has a single 'verdict' field; generate-html.js tallies by single verdict. Adding dual labels is a schema change beyond the scope of this proposal.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the dual-label proposal. WIN-016 stays 'Refuted by Data' (Gaia microarcsecond precision is overwhelming). Cite cascade only as a supplementary cross-reference in the detail_evidence, not as a verdict change. The proposal's own risks section already notes this as the fallback — make the fallback the only option.

holes_found.description: The proposal correctly identifies that WIN-068 (eclipse magnetic ensemble) has a lossy swap between 'Std Model Explains' (Chapman 1933) and 'Self-Contradicted' (cascade eclipse geometry paradox). The proposal leaves the call to curmudgeon.
holes_found.my_call: Keep 'Std Model Explains.' Chapman 1933 is one of the most-cited eclipse-magnetism mechanisms in standard geophysics; dropping it for a cascade argument that depends on unresolved numerics is net-negative. Cite cascade as supplementary (one sentence in detail_evidence) but do not flip the verdict.
holes_found.recommendation: WIN-068 stays 'Std Model Explains.' Add cascade as supplementary only.

holes_found.description: I agree with the analyst that 'cascade_dependent' beats 'v51_cascade' on version-agnosticism grounds. If the schema is changed per my hole-5 recommendation to 'cascade_role' enum, this becomes moot.
holes_found.recommendation: If single boolean: use cascade_dependent (analyst is right). If enum (my preference): use cascade_role with values 'primary'|'secondary'|null.

my_recommended_disposition.WIN-010: REJECT — wrong topic (eclipse magnetometer, not sun altitude)
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-016: STAY PUT — Refuted by Data (Gaia); cascade as supplementary footnote only
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-017: REJECT — wrong topic (stellar parallax, not surveying)
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-018: STAY PUT pending topic verification — likely remains Misleading (globe analemma adoption)
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-019: STAY PUT pending topic verification — likely remains Misleading (globe analemma adoption)
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-026: STAY PUT — Refuted by Data (convergent rays); cascade does not reach this argument
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-044: STAY PUT — Misleading (internal derivation); cascade has no linkage to FSF
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-046: STAY PUT — already Self-Contradicted; ADD cascade cross-reference as supplementary
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-056: STAY PUT — already Self-Contradicted; ANCHOR for Section 1.7 (walk reader through the collision ⇒ fix ⇒ cascade sequence)
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-065: STAY PUT — Refuted by Data (Polaris); cascade as supplementary only (proposal already conservative here)
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-067: STAY PUT — already Self-Contradicted; ADD cascade cross-reference as supplementary
my_recommended_disposition.WIN-068: STAY PUT — Std Model Explains (Chapman); cascade as supplementary only

flip_table_revised_summary.note: Under my recommended disposition, the cascade is load-bearing argumentatively (it explains why multiple existing verdicts are CORRECT and provides a unified structural frame) but does NOT change any verdicts in wins.json. This is a weaker-looking result than the analyst's 'up to 10 new Self-Contradicted' count, but it is the honest one. The strength of the cascade is its argumentative unification, not its verdict-flipping power.

kernel_of_truth.description: The V51.1 refraction cascade is a genuine and load-bearing structural critique. The underlying geometric observation — that the dome's firmament ceiling function H(r) and stated sun altitude cannot coexist, and that any patch via aetheric refraction creates a demand for refraction angles orders of magnitude larger than real atmospheric physics — is real, reproducible from dome source files, and editorially valuable. The analyst is correct that this deserves its own section and correct that Part 1 is the right home. The analyst is also correct (in the 'the_other_side' section) that the strongest dome defense is the moving-target critique and that the response must be structural framing.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_the_proposal_as_submi: The cascade's genuine strength has been oversold by the verdict-flip table. Rather than strengthening the critique, the proposed flips would WEAKEN it by attaching the cascade to WINs it does not actually reach (010, 017, 026, 044) or by replacing strong standalone arguments (Gaia for 016, Chapman for 068) with a single unified argument whose numerics are not yet verified. A good cascade section can exist without any verdict flips at all — and the revised disposition I recommend above lets the cascade do its real work (unifying six existing Self-Contradicted / Refuted verdicts into one structural frame) without making false promises.

gating_conditions_for_prose_writing: 1. Analyst re-reads every proposed-flip WIN's current detail_evidence and confirms topic match. Drops WIN-010, 017, 026, 044 from the flip roster entirely. Downgrades 016, 018, 019, 065, 068 to supplementary cross-reference only. Re-submits the revised flip table as a short addendum to this proposal package.
2. Analyst decides on tag schema: cascade_dependent boolean OR cascade_role enum (I recommend enum). Updates wins.json schema plan accordingly.

3. Analyst produces EXP-050-numerics.json companion with line-by-line reproduction of 13-27°, 400-675x, 83%, 3-8x from raw-text/ and dome formulas. Curmudgeon must be able to recompute each number independently from the source files cited.
4. Analyst produces a one-paragraph Section 1.5 vs. Section 1.7 split-statement.
5. Analyst produces a ~150-200 word Section 7 kill-shot card content plan (the hybrid option).
6. Analyst restructures the Section 1.7 outline so Point 1 is the geometric-invariant framing, not the V51.1-specific framing.
7. All of the above routed back to curmudgeon for a second proposal-review run BEFORE any prose is written or any wins.json patches are produced.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Cycle 3+ advocate mode is not strictly required for proposal reviews, but the proposal itself contains a 'the_other_side' section with four dome defenses. I add one: 'This proposal review is itself strengthening the dome by forcing you to delete verdict flips and downgrade the cascade section to a non-flipping unification. You started with an ambitious twelve-WIN cascade and now you have zero verdict changes. Isn't that a retreat?'
advocate_mode.analyst_response_if_challenged: The measure of a structural critique is not how many individual verdicts it changes but how tightly it frames the existing evidence. Six supplementary cross-references into a single structural argument is a unification, not a retreat. Adding false flips (WIN-010 as a sun-altitude claim when it is actually an eclipse magnetometer measurement) would make the review DEMONSTRABLY wrong — a strictly worse outcome than a conservative cascade section. The cascade's power is that it shows the dome's V51.1 patch has four internal contradictions with one geometric root; that argument is intact and load-bearing regardless of verdict count.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Section 1.7 should state explicitly in its closing paragraph: 'This cascade does not by itself flip any verdicts — it unifies the structural basis for six rebuttals already made on independent grounds. Its force lies in showing that these rebuttals are not a list of complaints but the predictable consequences of one geometric constraint.'

cross_win_consistency.note: These three already-Self-Contradicted WINs are the natural anchors for Section 1.7 because their existing rebuttals already lean on dome-internal geometric contradictions. Section 1.7 should explicitly cross-reference them and they should back-reference it. No inconsistency.

claims_checked: none in this review — deferred to EXP-050-numerics.json companion file
quantitative_verification.computations: N/A — proposal package contains approximate ranges only. Verification is the analyst's follow-up responsibility and a gating condition for my sign-off.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Cannot be assessed until companion numerics file is produced.

citations_unchecked: Meeus Astronomical Algorithms ch. 16 (0.5° max refraction) — correct as a textbook reference but should cite page/edition in final prose
Gaia DR3 1.8B star astrometry — already cited elsewhere in the review, consistent
TPXO/FES2014 tidal data — already cited elsewhere, consistent
NASA Espenak eclipse predictions — standard reference, consistent
Dome inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py — already cited in Part 2b, consistent
citation_check.note: No citations to verify in the proposal package itself — it contains proposed sources, not drafted prose. Citation verification happens on the prose draft.

deeper_analysis:
The proposal package demonstrates exactly the pattern curmudgeon reviews are meant to catch: a genuinely strong analytical insight (the cascade IS real and load-bearing) packaged with an over-ambitious deployment plan (twelve flips, some based on unverified WIN topics) and under-verified numerics (the 13-27° angle is the linchpin and it is not reproduced anywhere in the package). The analyst's own 'risks' section acknowledges several of these gaps honestly, which is credit-worthy. The job of this review is to convert those acknowledged risks into hard gates: no prose writing until the risks are actually addressed, not just flagged.

The deeper reason this proposal should not proceed as submitted is that the strength of a structural argument is brittle to factual errors at the edges. If Section 1.7 ships with WIN-010 listed as cascade-dependent and a dome defender notices that WIN-010 is a magnetometer measurement, the cascade's credibility takes damage disproportionate to the error — a single wrong flip makes the reader suspect every flip. The conservative path (no verdict flips, supplementary cross-references only, tight numerics) is strictly better than the ambitious path (twelve flips, some wrong) because the ambitious path's failure mode is credibility loss across the whole review.

On the other side of the argument (per user preferences): the analyst is RIGHT that a cascade section of some form belongs in Part 1, RIGHT that the moving-target defense is the strongest dome counter, RIGHT to defer prose writing until curmudgeon signs off, and RIGHT about the structural_placement and kernel_of_truth. The proposal's errors are all in the flip table and numerics, not in the meta-decisions. A revised proposal that drops the flip ambitions and tightens the numerics should sail through a second curmudgeon review.
